metadata.name: ww-input-checkbox
metadata.description: A form-integrated checkbox input component that provides a complete checkbox implementation with form validation, state management, and accessibility support. It wraps a native HTML checkbox with optional custom visual checkbox element and embedded label container.
features: Form validation and submission support with required field enforcement
Accessibility compliance with native HTML checkbox input
Customizable label positioning through embedded containers (left, right, top, bottom)
Automatic checked and readonly state management
Does not support checkbox groups or multi-select patterns natively

properties.name: initValue
properties.description: The initial checked state of the checkbox. When true, the checkbox is checked by default.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the checkbox must be checked for form validation to pass.

properties.name: readonly
properties.description: Whether the checkbox is in read-only mode, preventing user interaction.

properties.name: isEmbeddedContainer
properties.description: Whether to display an embedded container (label area) alongside the checkbox.

properties.name: containerPosition
properties.description: Position of the embedded container relative to the checkbox. Inactive when isEmbeddedContainer is false.

properties.name: containerGap
properties.description: Gap spacing between the checkbox and the embedded container. Accepts values in px, em, or rem units. Inactive when isEmbeddedContainer is false.

properties.name: fieldName
properties.description: The field name used to identify this checkbox in form submissions. Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container.

inactiveProperty.description: Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container (sidePanelContent.form?.uid is falsy).

properties.name: customValidation
properties.description: Enable custom validation rules for this checkbox field. Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container.

inactiveProperty.description: Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container (sidePanelContent.form?.uid is falsy).

properties.name: validation
properties.description: A formula expression used for custom validation logic. Only used when customValidation is enabled. Inactive when the element is not in a form container or when customValidation is false.

inactiveProperty.description: Inactive when the element is not placed inside a form container (sidePanelContent.form?.uid is falsy) or when customValidation is false.

states.name: checked
states.description: Active when the checkbox is in the checked state. Automatically managed based on the current value.

states.name: readonly
states.description: Active when the checkbox is in read-only mode. Automatically managed based on the readonly property.

events.name: change
events.description: Triggered when the user manually toggles the checkbox by clicking or interacting with it.

events.name: initValueChange
events.description: Triggered when the initial value property changes programmatically (not from user interaction).

slots.name: checkbox
slots.description: The visual checkbox element slot. Must exclusively contain a ww-checkbox component. DO NOT use ww-div, ww-icon, or any other component here.

slots.name: embeddedContainer
slots.description: An optional embedded container for label content displayed alongside the checkbox. Only visible when isEmbeddedContainer is true.

variables.name: value
variables.description: The current checked state of the checkbox as a boolean. Updated on user interaction or programmatic changes.

specifications: The checkbox slot must exclusively contain a ww-checkbox component. DO NOT use ww-div, ww-icon, or any other component.
The component automatically manages 'checked' and 'readonly' states - do not manually manage these through conditions.
The icon color property on the inner ww-checkbox controls only the checkmark appearance, not the background.
Default icon color should be transparent (#00000000) to hide the checkmark when unchecked, and use a contrasting color like white (#FFFFFF) for the checked state.
Background styling (backgroundColor, border) should be applied to the ww-checkbox via styles, NOT the color prop.
When using embedded containers, set isEmbeddedContainer to true and configure containerPosition and containerGap.
Text labels and descriptions should be placed in the embeddedContainer slot, not in the checkbox slot.
Styles must use named CSS properties (e.g., cursor, display, alignItems). Never use numeric indices or array-like keys in style objects.

examples.description: Basic checkbox with a label on the right side
